circuit.name: Nelson Piquet
circuit.history: The Nelson Piquet circuit (formerly Jacarepaguá) in Rio de Janeiro hosted 9 F1 World Championship Grands Prix between 1978 and 1988.
facts: First Grand Prix here: 1978, 9 World Championship GPs held coming into this season.
Most wins here: Alain Prost (5).
Most pole positions: Ayrton Senna (2).
Most successful constructor: McLaren (4 wins).
3 different winners in 9 Grands Prix.
Race lap record on this layout: 1:32.943 - Gerhard Berger (1988).

circuit.name: Enzo e Dino Ferrari
circuit.history: The Enzo e Dino Ferrari circuit (formerly Autodromo Dino Ferrari) in Imola hosted 9 F1 World Championship Grands Prix between 1980 and 1988.
facts: First Grand Prix here: 1980, 9 World Championship GPs held coming into this season.
Most wins here: Alain Prost / Nelson Piquet (2 each).
Most pole positions: Ayrton Senna (4).
Most successful constructor: McLaren (3 wins).
7 different winners in 9 Grands Prix.
Race lap record on this layout: 1:28.667 - Nelson Piquet (1986).
